agents.name: QA Engineer
learning_sequence: Team testing strategy: pyramid (unit/integration/E2E)
How to run the E2E suite locally: exact command and environment variables
Critical flows that must never break: login, registration, checkout
Test naming conventions and describe/it block organization
Bug reporting process in Jira: severity levels and how to attach evidence
Test code review process: who to pair test with

ticket_criteria.junior: Simple happy path E2E flow using the team's existing framework. Should not touch CI or configuration. Estimated time: 4-6 hours.
ticket_criteria.mid: E2E test of a complete business flow including edge cases. May require adding a helper or fixture. Estimated time: 6-10 hours.
ticket_criteria.senior: Design and implement the testing strategy for a new feature. Includes deciding what test levels are needed. Estimated time: 1-2 days.

interview_questions.stack_detection: What E2E testing framework do you have the most experience with — Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, or another?
interview_questions.depth: Have you written API tests in addition to E2E? Which tools have you used?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team uses {team_stack}. Have you worked with that before?
interview_questions.learning_style: How do you prefer to learn something new — reading docs, looking at code, or jumping straight in?
interview_questions.expectations: What do you hope to have accomplished in your first 30 days as a QA here?
interview_questions.adaptive: How long do you think you would need to get up to speed with {missing_tool}?

agents.name: DevOps / SRE
learning_sequence: Cloud architecture diagram: VPCs, subnets, main services
How the CI/CD pipeline works: from merge to staging deploy
Runbooks for the most common incidents: the 5 most frequent from last month
Team Terraform conventions: module structure, naming, state management
On-call process: tools, escalation, post-mortems
Gradual production access: buddy system protocol for the first 14 days

ticket_criteria.mid: Observability task in staging: add a Datadog alert or review/improve an existing dashboard. No infra modification. Time: 4-8 hours.
ticket_criteria.senior: Add a small Terraform module in staging: a security group, an S3 bucket with policies, or similar. Requires senior PR review. Time: 1 day.
ticket_criteria.staff: Review and document the high-availability architecture of a critical service. Identify improvements and propose a plan. Time: 2-3 days.

interview_questions.stack_detection: Which cloud provider do you have the most experience with — AWS, GCP, or Azure? And which IaC tool?
interview_questions.depth: Have you managed Kubernetes clusters in production or mainly in staging/dev?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team uses {team_stack}. Have you worked with that before?
interview_questions.learning_style: Do you prefer to understand the overall architecture first or dive straight into the most critical systems?
interview_questions.expectations: Which areas of DevOps do you feel you have the most room to grow in?
interview_questions.adaptive: For {missing_tool}, how long do you estimate you would need to feel comfortable using it in staging?

agents.name: Backend Developer
learning_sequence: General architecture: services, how they communicate, what each one does
How to run the server locally: complete setup of environment variables and DB
How to make a test request: team's Postman collection
Code conventions: linting, formatting, error handling patterns
How to view logs: structured logging, what to look for when something fails
PR process: what they review, approval criteria, who does the review

ticket_criteria.junior: Add validation for a missing field in an existing endpoint + the corresponding unit test. Touches real business code without risk. Time: 4-6 hours.
ticket_criteria.mid: New small endpoint with its validation, business logic, and tests. May require a DB schema change. Time: 1 day.
ticket_criteria.senior: Design and propose the technical solution for a new feature before implementing. Includes ADR if applicable. Time: 2-3 days.
ticket_criteria.lead: Identify and propose performance or architecture improvements in the main service. Lead the implementation. Time: 1 week.

interview_questions.stack_detection: What backend language and framework do you have the most recent experience with?
interview_questions.depth: Have you designed APIs from scratch or mainly extended existing ones?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team works mainly with {team_stack}. Do you have experience with that?
interview_questions.learning_style: Do you prefer to understand the data model first or the end-to-end request flow?
interview_questions.expectations: What type of backend work are you most comfortable with — new features, performance optimization, or refactoring?
interview_questions.adaptive: For {missing_tool}, have you worked with something similar that might help you ramp up faster?

agents.name: AI / ML Engineer
learning_sequence: AI system architecture: which models are in production, how they are served
Data pipeline: how data arrives, how it is processed, what is anonymized
How to run an experiment: MLflow, naming conventions, metrics logging
Evaluation stack: how models are evaluated before going to production
Model deployment process: required approvals, rollback procedure
Data and privacy policies: which data can be used for training and which cannot

ticket_criteria.junior: Replicate an existing experiment with a different dataset and document the results in MLflow. Pure experimentation, no production access. Time: 1-2 days.
ticket_criteria.mid: Improve the evaluation pipeline of an existing model: add a new metric or automate a manual test. Time: 2-3 days.
ticket_criteria.senior: Review and propose improvements to the current RAG pipeline: identify where it is losing recall or precision. Include technical proposal. Time: 3-4 days.

interview_questions.stack_detection: What is your experience with LLMs — have you worked more with fine-tuning, RAG/prompting, or model deployment/serving?
interview_questions.depth: Have you taken ML models to production or is your experience mainly in research/experimentation?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team works mainly with {team_stack}. Have you used that before?
interview_questions.learning_style: Do you prefer to start by understanding the business problem the model solves, or dive straight into the code?
interview_questions.expectations: Which part of the ML lifecycle are you strongest in — data, modeling, evaluation, or deployment?
interview_questions.adaptive: For {missing_tool}, have you worked with a similar tool that gives you a foundation to learn quickly?

agents.name: Frontend Developer
learning_sequence: Component structure and design tokens of the design system
How to run the project locally and Storybook
How routing and state management work
Code conventions: naming, component structure, props typing
Code review process: what they review, how to connect with the design team
Deploy: how to create a preview deployment and the process to production

ticket_criteria.junior: Update a UI component to support a new design system variant. Requires reading the design tokens and writing 1 snapshot test. Time: 3-5 hours.
ticket_criteria.mid: Implement a new more complex component with its responsive variant, states, and tests. Connected to a real API. Time: 1-2 days.
ticket_criteria.senior: Identify and resolve a performance issue in the bundle or re-renders of a critical component. Include benchmarks. Time: 2-3 days.

interview_questions.stack_detection: What frontend framework do you have the most experience with — React, Vue, Angular, or another?
interview_questions.depth: Have you worked with TypeScript deeply or mainly with lightly typed JavaScript?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team uses {team_stack}. Do you have experience with that?
interview_questions.learning_style: Do you prefer to start by understanding the design system or go straight to how the frontend connects to the APIs?
interview_questions.expectations: Which area of frontend are you strongest in — components/UI, state management, or performance/tooling?
interview_questions.adaptive: For {missing_tool}, have you worked with something in the same category that gives you a foundation?

agents.name: Data Engineer
learning_sequence: Data warehouse architecture: raw/staging/mart layers and their conventions
Most-used table catalog: what each one contains, who the owner is
How to run and test a dbt model locally: commands and naming conventions
How the main Airflow DAG works: what it orchestrates, SLAs, how to view logs
Data quality process: what tests exist, how to add new ones
Customer data access: privacy policy, what is anonymized and how

ticket_criteria.junior: Add a data quality test to an existing dbt model: not_null, unique, accepted_values. Staging read-only. Time: 4-6 hours.
ticket_criteria.mid: Create a new dbt model in the mart layer that answers a specific business question. Includes documentation and tests. Time: 1-2 days.
ticket_criteria.senior: Identify and resolve a performance issue in a critical pipeline: optimize a slow dbt model or an inefficient Airflow DAG. Time: 2-3 days.

interview_questions.stack_detection: What data transformation tool do you have the most experience with — dbt, Spark, pandas, or another?
interview_questions.depth: Have you worked with streaming pipelines or mainly batch processing?
interview_questions.gap_detection: The team mainly uses {team_stack}. Have you worked with that?
interview_questions.learning_style: Do you prefer to understand the warehouse architecture first or go straight to how a pipeline is built?
interview_questions.expectations: Which part of data work are you strongest in — pipelines, modeling, data quality, or visualization?
interview_questions.adaptive: For {missing_tool}, do you have experience with similar tools that would help you ramp up quickly?
